channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 2 earthquakes in Venezuela kill at least 164. And, Trump cancels housing bill signing
item.description: Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.
item.content:encoded:
Two major earthquakes in Venezuela have killed at least 164 people and left hundreds injured. And, President Trump canceled the signing of a massive bipartisan housing bill yesterday.

(Image credit: Manaure Quintero)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Long before the World Cup, Ukrainian immigrants built a soccer powerhouse in Philly
item.description: World Cup games are underway in Philadelphia. Long before Americans caught the world's soccer craze, Ukrainian migrants made Philly a soccer town. Today, the sport helps sustain their culture.
item.content:encoded:
World Cup games are underway in Philadelphia. Long before Americans caught the world's soccer craze, Ukrainian migrants made Philly a soccer town. Today, the sport helps sustain their culture.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Trump keeps sabotaging legislation over a voting bill. Here's what's in it
item.description: President Trump blew up what could have been a win for his party — and he did it to force lawmakers to pass an elections overhaul bill that has been all but doomed in the Senate.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump blew up what could have been a win for his party — and he did it to force lawmakers to pass an elections overhaul bill that has been all but doomed in the Senate.

(Image credit: Laura Brett)
item.dc:creator: Ashley Lopez

item.title: How to find middle ground when your partner wants kids — and you don't
item.description: Psychotherapist Merle Bombardieri has been helping couples with this conundrum for decades. She shares four exercises to bring clarity to the situation — and find a solution that minimizes regret.
item.content:encoded: Psychotherapist Merle Bombardieri has been helping couples with this conundrum for decades. She shares four exercises to bring clarity to the situation — and find a solution that minimizes regret.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: 4 surprising things to know about abortion in America since Dobbs
item.description: A confusing patchwork of state laws began to take shape hours after the Supreme Court ruled to overturn Roe v. Wade on June 24, 2022. Here's where things stand now on the abortion issue.
item.content:encoded:
. '/>

A confusing patchwork of state laws began to take shape hours after the Supreme Court ruled to overturn Roe v. Wade on June 24, 2022. Here's where things stand now on the abortion issue.

(Image credit: Jacquelyn Martin)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: 'They can kill you': Immigrants fear a surge in xenophobic violence in South Africa
item.description: Foreign-owned businesses have been attacked, migrants driven from their homes, and several killed. A leading xenophobic group has given all undocumented immigrants until June 30 to leave the country.
item.content:encoded:
Foreign-owned businesses have been attacked, migrants driven from their homes, and several killed. A leading xenophobic group has given all undocumented immigrants until June 30 to leave the country.

(Image credit: Themba Hadebe)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: Eager for a break, farmers don't expect much relief after the Iran peace deal
item.description: Fertilizer prices have gone down with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the prospect of a U.S.-Iran peace deal. But struggling American farmers won't likely see any relief for months.
item.content:encoded:
Fertilizer prices have gone down with the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the prospect of a U.S.-Iran peace deal. But struggling American farmers won't likely see any relief for months.

(Image credit: Kirk Siegler)
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: When going to 'camp' meant Roman military lodgings — not summer fun
item.description: In this installment of NPR's Word of the Week, we go to camp: from 16th-century military lodgings to the wilderness adventures of the 1880s designed to turn boys into "manly men."
item.content:encoded:
In this installment of NPR's Word of the Week, we go to camp: from 16th-century military lodgings to the wilderness adventures of the 1880s designed to turn boys into "manly men."

(Image credit: Joshua Bickel)
item.dc:creator: Natalie Escobar

item.title: Trump is pushing to institutionalize homeless people. That may include veterans
item.description: A Trump executive order pushes involuntary treatment for homeless people; the VA denies that would include homeless vets.
item.content:encoded:
A Trump executive order pushes involuntary treatment for homeless people; the VA denies that would include homeless vets.

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: Norman Rockwell art that ignited a lawsuit and a love story is now on public view
item.description: So You Want to See The President! depicts a procession of visitors waiting to see Franklin Delano Roosevelt. The original 1943 Rockwell suite of illustrations goes on public view Thursday in D.C.
item.content:encoded:
So You Want to See the President! Click to enlarge.'/>

So You Want to See The President! depicts a procession of visitors waiting to see Franklin Delano Roosevelt. The original 1943 Rockwell suite of illustrations goes on public view Thursday in D.C.

(Image credit: Bruce M. White)
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair
